structured_payload_candidate.title: Zone Minimum Outdoor Air Calculation for Standard 62.1
structured_payload_candidate.section_title: For compliance with the Ventilation Rate Procedure of ASHRAE Standard 62.1-2016
structured_payload_candidate.summary: For compliance with ASHRAE Standard 62.1, the required zone outdoor airflow Voz is calculated as Voz = (Vbz-A* + Vbz-P*)/Ez, where Vbz-A* and Vbz-P* are modified based on zone conditions (e.g., unoccupied, window open, occupancy sensor, CO2 sensor).
structured_payload_candidate.trigger_condition: Zone is in Occupied Mode or other modes with specific conditions
structured_payload_candidate.required_behavior: Calculate Voz = (Vbz-A* + Vbz-P*)/Ez. Modify Vbz-A* and Vbz-P* per priority: 1) If zone not in Occupied Mode or window open: Vbz-P*=0, Vbz-A*=0, Vmin*=0. 2) If occupancy sensor and unpopulated with occupied-standby permitted: Vbz-P*=0, Vbz-A*=0, Vmin*=0. 3) If occupancy sensor and unpopulated without occupied-standby: Vbz-P*=0, Vmin*=Vmin. 4) If CO2 sensor: use P-only loop to reset Vmin* and Vbz-P*.
structured_payload_candidate.evidence_quote: The required zone outdoor airflow Voz shall be calculated as Voz = (Vbz-A* + Vbz-P*)/Ez, where the normal values of Vbz-A* and Vbz-P* are modified if any of the following conditions are met, in order from higher to lower priority: 1. If the zone is in any mode other than Occupied Mode, and for zones that have window switches and the window is open: Vbz-P* = 0, Vbz-A* = 0, and Vmin* = 0. 2. If the zone has an occupancy sensor, is unpopulated, and occupied-standby mode is permitted: Vbz-P* = 0, Vbz-A* = 0, and Vmin* = 0. 3. Else, if the zone has an occupancy sensor, is unpopulated, but occupied-standby mode is not permitted: Vbz-P* = 0 and Vmin* = Vmin. 4. If the zone has a CO2 sensor: ...

structured_payload_candidate.title: Zone Minimum Airflow Setpoint Vmin Determination for Standard 62.1
structured_payload_candidate.section_title: For compliance with the Ventilation Rate Procedure of ASHRAE Standard 62.1-2016
structured_payload_candidate.summary: Vmin is set to Voz if Vmin is AUTO and the AHU has been supplying 100% OA for 10 minutes; otherwise set to 1.5*Voz if AUTO and not 100% OA; otherwise use entered Vmin.
structured_payload_candidate.trigger_condition: Vmin in Section 3.1.2 is 'AUTO' or a fixed value
structured_payload_candidate.required_behavior: If Vmin is 'AUTO' and AHU has been supplying 100% OA for 10 minutes, Vmin = Voz. Else if Vmin is 'AUTO' and AHU is not supplying 100% OA, Vmin = 1.5 * Voz. Else Vmin = entered value.
structured_payload_candidate.evidence_quote: Vmin 1. Shall be equal to Voz as calculated in Section 5.2.1.3.f below if Vmin in Section 3.1.2 is 'AUTO' and the associated air handler has been supplying 100% outdoor air (outdoor air damper fully open; return air damper fully closed) for 10 minutes; 2. Else shall be equal to 1.5 * Voz as calculated in Section 5.2.1.3.f below if Vmin in Section 3.1.2 is 'AUTO' and the associated air handler is not supplying 100% outdoor air; 3. Else shall be equal Vmin as entered in Section 3.1.2.

structured_payload_candidate.title: CO2 DCV for Standard 62.1 - Cooling-Only, Reheat, Series Fan-Powered, Dual-Duct Terminal Units
structured_payload_candidate.section_title: For compliance with the Ventilation Rate Procedure of ASHRAE Standard 62.1-2016
structured_payload_candidate.equipment_scope: Cooling-only VAV, reheat VAV, constant-volume series fan-powered, dual-duct VAV terminal units with mixing control and inlet airflow sensors, dual-duct VAV with mixing control and discharge airflow sensor, or dual-duct VAV with cold-duct minimum control
structured_payload_candidate.summary: During Occupied Mode, a P-only loop maintains CO2 at setpoint, resetting from 0% at setpoint minus 200 ppm to 100% at setpoint. The loop output resets Vmin* from Vmin to Vcool-max and Vbz-P* from 0 to Vbz-P in parallel.
structured_payload_candidate.trigger_condition: Zone has CO2 sensor and is in Occupied Mode
structured_payload_candidate.required_behavior: Enable P-only CO2 control loop. Reset Vmin* from Vmin (0% output) to Vcool-max (100% output). Reset Vbz-P* from 0 (0% output) to Vbz-P (100% output). Disable loop when not in Occupied Mode.
structured_payload_candidate.evidence_quote: During Occupied Mode, a P-only loop shall maintain CO2 concentration at setpoint; reset from 0% at setpoint minus 200 PPM and to 100% at setpoint. ... The CO2 control loop output shall reset both the occupied minimum airflow setpoint (Vmin*) and the population component of the required breathing zone outdoor airflow (Vbz-P*) in parallel. Vmin* shall be reset from the zone minimum airflow setpoint Vmin at 0% loop output up to maximum cooling airflow setpoint Vcool-max at 100% loop output. Vbz-P* shall be reset from 0 L/s (0 cfm) at 0% loop output up to the Vbz-P at 100% loop output.

structured_payload_candidate.title: CO2 DCV for Standard 62.1 - Parallel Fan-Powered Terminal Units
structured_payload_candidate.section_title: For compliance with the Ventilation Rate Procedure of ASHRAE Standard 62.1-2016
structured_payload_candidate.summary: During Occupied Mode, a P-only CO2 loop resets Vmin* from Vmin to VCO2-max and Vbz-P* from 0 to Vbz-P. VCO2-max equals Vcool-max when cooling, or Vcool-max minus parallel fan airflow when heating or deadband.
structured_payload_candidate.trigger_condition: Zone has CO2 sensor and is in Occupied Mode
structured_payload_candidate.required_behavior: Determine VCO2-max: if Zone State is cooling, VCO2-max = Vcool-max; if heating or deadband, VCO2-max = Vcool-max minus parallel fan airflow. Reset Vmin* from Vmin (0% output) to VCO2-max (100% output). Reset Vbz-P* from 0 to Vbz-P.
structured_payload_candidate.evidence_quote: Determine VCO2-max as follows: (1) When the Zone State is cooling, VCO2-max is equal to the maximum cooling airflow setpoint Vcool-max. (2) When the Zone State is heating or deadband, VCO2-max is equal to Vcool-max minus the parallel fan airflow ... The CO2 control loop output shall reset both the occupied minimum airflow setpoint Vmin* and the population component of the required breathing zone outdoor airflow Vbz-P* in parallel. Vmin* shall be reset from the zone minimum airflow setpoint Vmin at 0% loop output up to maximum cooling airflow setpoint VCO2-max at 100% loop output. Vbz-P* shall be reset from 0 L/s (0 cfm) at 0% loop output up to the Vbz-P at 100% loop output.

structured_payload_candidate.title: Zone Minimum Outdoor Air Calculation for California Title 24
structured_payload_candidate.summary: Zone-Abs-OA-min and Zone-Des-OA-min are determined based on priority conditions: window open, occupancy sensor, CO2 sensor, or default. Vmin equals Zone-Abs-OA-min if AUTO, else entered Vmin. Vmin* equals Vmin except with occupancy sensor or window open (zero) or CO2 sensor (reset by loop).
structured_payload_candidate.trigger_condition: Zone is in Occupied Mode or other modes with specific conditions
structured_payload_candidate.required_behavior: Determine Zone-Abs-OA-min and Zone-Des-OA-min per priority. Set Vmin = Zone-Abs-OA-min if AUTO, else entered Vmin. Set Vmin* = Vmin except: if occupancy sensor and occupied-standby permitted and unpopulated, Vmin*=0; if window open, Vmin*=0; if CO2 sensor, reset Vmin* via P-only loop.
structured_payload_candidate.evidence_quote: Zone-Abs-OA-min shall be reset based on the following conditions in order from highest to lowest priority: i. Zero if the zone has an occupancy sensor and is unpopulated and is permitted to be in occupied-standby mode per Section 3.1.1.2.b.3. iii. Varea-min if the zone has a CO2 sensor. iv. Zone-Des-OA-min otherwise. ... Vmin 1. Shall be equal to Zone-Abs-OA-min if Vmin in Section 3.1.2 is 'AUTO'; 2. Else shall be equal to Vmin as entered in Section 3.1.2. ... The occupied minimum airflow Vmin* shall be equal to Vmin except as noted below, in order from highest to lowest priority: 1. If the zone has an occupancy sensor and is permitted to be in occupied-standby mode per Section 3.1.1.2.b.3, Vmin* shall be equal to zero when the room is unpopulated. 2. If the zone has a window switch, Vmin* shall be zero when the window is open. 3. If the zone has a CO2 sensor: ...

structured_payload_candidate.title: Time-Averaged Ventilation (TAV) Pulse Width Modulation
structured_payload_candidate.summary: When the active airflow setpoint Vspt is nonzero and less than the lowest possible setpoint Vm, the airflow setpoint is pulse width modulated with a total cycle time of 15 minutes (adjustable). Open period OP is the larger of 1.5 minutes or TCT * TAVratio. Closed period CP = TCT - OP. During closed period, damper control loop disabled and output set to 0. At end of closed period, damper commanded to last position from previous open period before reenabling loop.
structured_payload_candidate.trigger_condition: Vspt is nonzero and less than Vm
structured_payload_candidate.required_behavior: Calculate TAVratio = Vspt/Vm. Set TCT = 15 minutes (adjustable). Open period OP = max(1.5 minutes, TCT * TAVratio). Closed period CP = TCT - OP. During open period, Vspt* = Vm. During closed period, Vspt* = 0, disable VAV damper control loop with output 0. At end of closed period, command damper to last position from previous open period, then reenable loop. Repeat until Vspt > Vm.
structured_payload_candidate.evidence_quote: When the active airflow setpoint Vspt is nonzero and is less than the lowest possible airflow setpoint allowed by the controls (Vm), the airflow setpoint shall be pulse width modulated as follows: a. The time-averaged ventilation (TAV) ratio shall be determined as TAVratio = Vspt/Vm b. The total cycle time (TCT) shall be 15 minutes (adjustable) c. Open period. During the open period, the TAV airflow setpoint Vspt* shall be equal to Vm for a period of time OP, which is the larger of the following: d. 1.5 minutes or e. TCT multiplied by TAVratio f. Closed period. During the closed period, Vspt* shall be set to 0 for a period of time CP, where CP = TCT – OP. The VAV damper control loop shall be disabled with output set equal to 0 during the closed period. At the end of each closed period, the VAV damper shall be commanded to the last position from the previous open period prior to reenabling the control loop. g. During TAV mode, each cycle shall consist of an open and closed period that alternate until Vspt is greater than Vm.
